\u5c71\u8109.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: The dangerous situation made the Stone City area one of the ancient battlefields in Nanjing. Since the Six Dynasties, many wars have been fought here. In the fourth year of Tianji (280), General Longxiang Wang Jun, the governor of Yizhou in Jin Dynasty, led a navy with large ships and sailed eastward from Wuchang Fujiang River. The Wu army set up iron cables across the river to block it, but the Jin soldiers used fire to fuse the iron cables and passed through three mountains to the stone city, destroying the Wu state. ——"The Origin of Nanjing Place Names"

\u5c71\u8109.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《建康实录》、《景定建康志》、《六朝事迹编类》、《至正金陵新志》
\u5c71\u8109.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: During the Six Dynasties, Jiangshan's geographical position was very important. "Danyang Ji" written by Shan Qian of Liu Song Dynasty in the Southern Dynasty records: "The north and south of the capital are connected by mountains, and Jiangshan is different. It looks like a dragon, which is actually the town of Yangdu." "The capital" and "Yangdu" both refer to Nanjing. "The town of Yangdu" means that Jiangshan is the town of Nanjing. ——"Nanjing's Names in the Past"

According to the "Illustrated Book": After the Western Region Monk Silamitra opened the mountain in the Eastern Jin Dynasty, Master Yunguang continued to lecture on the Lotus Sutra in the Liang Tianjian year, and it rained precious flowers from the sky, so it was named its platform. \u5c71\u8109.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: After the Eastern Jin Dynasty, during the Southern Dynasties (420-589 AD) of the Song, Qi, Liang and Chen dynasties, the capital of Jiankang further developed and prospered. By the time of Emperor Wu of Liang Dynasty (early 6th century AD), the surrounding suburbs of Jiankang stretched from Nitang (near Fangshan in present-day Jiangning County) to the east, Shitou City to the west, Shizigang to the south, and Purple Mountain to the north, with an area of 20 kilometers each in the east, west, and south. With 280,000 residents and a population of more than 1 million, it was the largest city in my country at that time. Inside and outside the capital of Jiankang, the rulers of the Southern Dynasties built more than 30 royal gardens and villas. Such as "Leyou Garden" centered on Fuzhou Mountain, "Hualin Garden" centered on Jilong Mountain, "Jianxing Garden" on the south bank of the Qinhuai River, "Fanglin Garden" and "Louhu Garden" on Qingxi River , "Shanglin Garden" on the north bank of Xuanwu Lake and "Qinglin Garden" on the east bank of the lake, "Fangshan Garden" on the side of Fangshan Mountain, "Bowang Garden" at the foot of Zhongshan Mountain, and "Lingqiu Garden" and "Lingqiu Garden" on the riverside. "Jiangtan Garden" and so on. Except for the scenic spots in the suburbs of Jiankang, almost all were occupied by the royal family. The largest ones were Leyou Garden and Hualin Garden. ——"Nanjing History"

\u5c71\u8109.\u5730\u7406\u4f4d\u7f6e: South Bank of the Yangtze River, Gulou District, Nanjing City

\u5c71\u8109.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Mufu Mountain is located in the north of Nanjing City, Jiangsu Province. After Emperor Jin and Yuan crossed the river, Prime Minister Wang Dao built the shogunate here, hence the name. The mountain is adjacent to the Yangtze River in the north, and the situation is dangerous. It was the gateway to Jiankang during the Eastern Jin and Southern Dynasties. At the end of the Liang Dynasty in the Southern Dynasty, the Northern Qi army crossed the river and marched south. Chen Baxian led his army out of the mountain to resist the war and defeated the Northern Qi army. The mountain has six ancient Jinling Forty-eight Scenic Spots. Because cultural relics engraved with "the first year of Wufeng (254)" and "Mo (Shogunate) Mountain" were unearthed near Guojiashan, some experts questioned whether the current Mufu Mountain is It is not the Shogunate Mountain during the Six Dynasties period. The mountain has Southern Dynasty celadon six-legged inkstones excavated from destroyed ancient tombs. The ancient tombs on the mountain are protected cultural relics in Nanjing.

\u5c71\u8109.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: During the Six Dynasties, Sheshan was the retreat place of the famous hermit Ming Zhengjun. Later, he donated his house and built a temple, which later led to the Qixia Temple. Ming Zhengjun, the founder of Qixia Temple, Ming Sengshao, was a famous hermit from the Liu Song Dynasty to the Xiao Qi period in the Southern Dynasties. The "Book of Southern Qi Gao Yi Biography" said that he "had Confucianism in Ming Sutra" and was a highly educated person. . He first hid in Laoshan, Shandong Province, gathering disciples to give lectures. Later, he followed his younger brother Ming Qingfu to Yanyu Mountain in Yuzhou (today's Lianyungang), where he "lived in Yun Jingshe, played with water and rocks, and never entered the state city." After Qingfu returned to the capital Jiankang in the Ming Dynasty, Seng Shao of the Ming Dynasty returned with him and lived in Sheshan. During this period, the emperor issued edicts many times to recruit him to serve as an official, but he always refused to accept the offer. This true hermit spirit of being indifferent, not seeking wealth, being clean and self-respecting, and strictly adhering to ethics won people's respect, so he then He has the reputation of "conquering the king". In the seventh year of Yongming in the Southern Qi Dynasty (489), Monk Shao of the Ming Dynasty donated his residence in Sheshan and built a temple. Because he was nicknamed "Qixia", the temple was called "Qixia Jingshe", which later became Qixia Temple and Sheshan. Hence the name Qixia Mountain. ——"Nanjing Architecture of the Ming and Qing Dynasties"

\u5c71\u8109.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Legend has it that Emperor Sima Rui of the Jin and Yuan Dynasties made his capital Jiankang. After the establishment of the Eastern Jin Dynasty, he wanted to build twin towers outside Xuanyang Gate. In order to avoid extensive construction work, Wang Dao pointed to the two peaks of Niu Shou and said: "This is the sky tower." So Sima Xiang gave up the construction. Que’s thoughts. There are also legends that during the Southern Dynasties, the eminent monk Pizhi lived in a cave on the south slope of Xifeng, where he "became a Buddha on the ground and an immortal in heaven", leaving behind a famous relic - the "Buddha Cave". In the Liang Dynasty of the Southern Dynasties, Buddhist cave temples were built here. ——"Nanjing Folk Customs"

\u5c71\u8109.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Qingliang Mountain in the Qing Dynasty was one of the secluded places for literati. Liu Zhi, Gong Xian, etc. all settled here. Liu Zhi's life was spent in reading and writing, which can be described as ordinary. However, how bitter and difficult is the life of a person who refuses favors, ignores his wife and children, and depends on others for everything, and depends on others for life! He did not tell future generations what specific experiences he experienced, but he sighed with tears: "The repetition of human hearts, the ruggedness of the world, the turmoil of the dust, the insults of reputation and blasphemy, the sun has no peaceful dial, and the heart has no peace!" "The Holy Sacred Heaven!" After the completion of "Record", he healed his traumatized heart and returned to Qingliang Mountain to rebuild the hut. After that, he resolutely quit writing, got rid of the troubles of the world, spent more than ten years of quiet time in the clear breeze and bright moon of Sweeping Leaf Tower, and passed away. ——"Celebrities and Nanjing"

\u5c71\u8109.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: The mountains and rivers of Fuzhoushan are quiet and quiet. During the Six Dynasties, it was a prosperous place for royal gardens, and many poems about banquets and feasts were left here. As early as the Soochow Dynasty, there was a Western Pool on the mountain, which was built by Prince Sun Deng and was called Taizi Lake. In the Eastern Jin Dynasty, the Beijiao Altar was set up at the foot of the southern part of the mountain and a medicine garden was opened. Later, "in Jiazhong of the Song and Yuan Dynasties, the land was called Beiyuan, a tower was built, and it was renamed Leyou Garden." Bao Zhao, a famous poet of the Liu Song Dynasty, wrote in his poem "Serving a Banquet: Covering Zhoushan": "The rain has stopped and the upper suburbs are clear, and the clouds are shining in the middle. You can visit the Xuan Yue Dan residence, and the bright candles gather in the Chun Palace." It is a portrayal of the prosperity at that time. ——"History of Nanjing Temples"

It is named after the hot spring. Princes of the Liang Dynasty bathed here and wrote poems in praise of it. It was developed and utilized earlier. Prince Zhaoming of the Liang Dynasty in the Southern Dynasties bathed here and recited poems in Biyang Valley. "Taiping Huanyu Ji" says: "Tangshan Mountain is in the northeast of the county, and it is connected to Yunxue Mountain to the west. The mountain is not very tall, and there are six places large and small with soup flowing out of it. The soup stream surrounds its southeast, and it is always hot all the time, with the color of a bird, etc. If you put it in, it will rot; if you cook the beans and grains, they will remain uncooked for a long time; if you touch the plants and trees, they will become fresh and lush." - "The Origin of Nanjing Place Names"

Sun Quan of the Eastern Wu Dynasty built the city with stones, which was also the starting point for Nanjing to become the "Jinling". According to historical records, when Sun Quan moved to Moling in the 17th year of Jian'an in the Han Dynasty (212), he built a city on Shitou Mountain in Nanjing, which was named "Stone City". Due to its long history, the city no longer exists, but there is still a dangerous cliff on the side of the original Stone Mountain near the river bank, with a mottled surface, commonly known as "Ghost Face City". ——"Nanjing Cultural Chronicles Volume 2"

\u5c71\u8109.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: “The best place to board in Nandu”
\u5c71\u8109.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: It was a strategic battle pass with dangerous terrain during the Six Dynasties period. Lion Rock was called Lulong Mountain in ancient times. It is said that when Sima Rui, Emperor of the Jin and Yuan Dynasties, first came to the south of the Yangtze River when he crossed the river, he saw the rolling mountains and rocks in the distance. He exclaimed: "It's really a pass on the river." It was named "Lulong Mountain". "Jingding Jiankang Zhi" records: Lulong Mountain has a circumference of 12 miles and is 36 feet (130 meters) high. There is water in the east and a flat land in the west. It faces the river in the west. It has the potential to build a high-rise building. It has been a strategic pass in wars throughout the ages. Guard Nanjing’s natural barrier. ——"The Origin of Nanjing Place Names"
The name Dingshan originated from the Liang Dynasty in the Southern Dynasties. Today's top mountain should be a homophony of "ding" and "ding". --Baidu Encyclopedia

\u5c71\u8109.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Zutang Mountain is located in the south of Niushou Mountain and west of Moling Street. It is about 255.9 meters high and is a branch of Niushou Mountain. Zutang Mountain was named Youqi Mountain in ancient times because of the Youqi Temple built on the mountain. At the beginning of the Tang Dynasty, Zen Master Farong attained enlightenment here and became the first founder of the Southern Sect of Buddhism. The mountain was also renamed Zutang Mountain. --Baidu Encyclopedia

In the next thirty years, Yu Zai Jiangning, the garden collapsed and slumped, the room was turned into a wine shop, and the public platform was noisy, the birds were disgusted and refused to crouch, the flowers withered, and the spring breeze could not bloom. ——"Suiyuan Notes"

\u53e4\u8ff9.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: During the Eastern Jin Dynasty, due to the surge in Jiankang's population, the industrial and commercial areas and residential areas were expanded compared to those during the Eastern Wu Dynasty. The original prosperous areas such as Changganli and Dashi were no longer sufficient, and development began to occur on both sides of the Qinhuai River and Qingxi in the east. Wuyi Lane, where wealthy families such as Wang and Xie live, is located to the east of Zhuquehang. This high-end residential area was originally the location of the "Wuyi Camp" of the Imperial Guard in Soochow. Later, Liu Yuxi, a poet of the Tang Dynasty, wrote in a poem titled "Woyi Alley": "There are wild grasses and flowers beside the Suzaku Bridge, and the setting sun is setting at the entrance of Wuyi Alley. In the old days, there were swallows in front of Wang Xietang, and flying people were ordinary people's homes." - "Nanjing" "Historical Stories"

\u6c34\u4f53.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Nanjing is located in the lower reaches of the Yangtze River and is the hub of water and land transportation in central China. It is connected to Shanghai, the largest seaport in the country, to the east. It goes up the river and can control the western provinces, including Sun Wu, Eastern Jin, Song, Qi, Liang, Chen, and Southern Tang. , the former capital of the Eight Dynasties of the early Ming Dynasty and the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom, and the military and political center of the reactionary rule of the Kuomintang for 20 years. Nanjing City consists of Nanjing City and suburbs such as Pukou and Tangshan. It is bounded by Wulongshan Outer Site and Tangshan to the east, Tiexinqiao, Xishanqiao and Dashengguan to the south, Jiangning County, the Yangtze River and Pukou Town to the west, Jiangpu County, and north It is bounded by the Yangtze River and Liuhe County. The city covers an area of 30 kilometers from east to west and 40 kilometers from north to south. According to the "Nanjing Statistical Quarterly" published by the pseudo-government government in June 1948, the total area is 559.276 square kilometers (838.915 City acres). Xiaguan is part of the urban area, located along the Yangtze River outside Yijiang Gate. It is the location of the ship terminal and the Beijing-Shanghai Railway Station, and has prosperous commerce. In 1858, the British and French invaders invaded Dagu and made the Treaty of Tianjin, which stipulated that Nanjing should be established as a commercial port. At that time, Nanjing was not implemented because it was the capital of the Taiping Revolutionary Army. In 1899, the Manchu Qing government opened the Xiaguan Pass and established the Jinling Pass, allowing further imperialist forces to invade. When the revolutionary army captured Nanjing in 1927, the American, British, and French imperialist warships stationed in Nanjing fired into the city, helping the Kuomintang reactionaries massacre the Chinese people. To this day, most of the Shimonoseki Pier is still in the hands of the imperialists. ——"Overview of Nanjing"

\u6c34\u4f53.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: At the end of the Han Dynasty, Sun Quan established the Kingdom of Wu and built the capital on the side of Fuzhou Mountain in the first year of Huanglong. It surrounds Pinggang in the east, Shitou in the west, Qinhuai in the front, Xuanwu in the north, and goes back twenty miles and nineteen steps. Its main entrance is called Xuanyang. (In today's Zhongzheng Street.) Five miles south to Huai River, there is Dahangmen. (In today's Changle Ferry.) In the tenth year of Chiwu, the Taichu Palace was built in it. It was 500 feet in circumference and had eight gates. The first five gates were called Bus, called Shengxian, Mingyang, Zuoye, and Youye. The east gate is called Qinglong; the west gate is called White Tiger; the back gate is called Xuanwu. They also built a large garden city behind the palace (between today's Han Mansion and Pearl Bridge.) This was the first foundation of the city. Since the Eastern Jin Dynasty, twelve gates have been opened according to the old system: Xuanyang in the south, called Kaiyang, Qingming in the east, Lingyang in the west; Dongyang in the east, Jianchun; Changhe in the west, Ximing; The north is called Xuanwu, it is called Daxia, it is called Guangmo, it is called Yanxi. The Wu Dahang Gate was renamed Zhuque, and the Yuan City was renovated into a new palace. The due south is called Da Sima Gate (located at today's Xihuamen Street), opposite the Xuanyang Gate of the capital; the north is called Pingchang Gate; (located at the south entrance of today's Chengxian Street.) The east and west gates are called Dongye and Xiye. There are Taiji Hall and Qingshu Hall inside. Later, Song, Qi, Liang and Chen all remained the same. However, the Yang gate was renamed Jinyang, the Lingyang gate was renamed Guangyang, the Guangmo gate was renamed Beiye, and Wanchun and Qianqiu gates were opened in the east and west of Taicheng, and later they were renamed Yunlong and Shenhu. In the Song Dynasty, there were Hanzhang, Mingyang, Fanngle and other halls. Qi and Liang have Chongyun, Wuming, Pixiang and other halls. Chen Ze has Jingyang Tower and three pavilions of Linchun, Jieqi and Wangxian, all in Taicheng. When the Sui Dynasty reached the south of the Yangtze River and built the city of Kang, the palaces and palaces were all cultivated into fields, and the relics of the Southern Dynasties were swept away. ——"Nine Types of Jinling Suozhi"

\u6c34\u4f53.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Emperor Wu of Liang lacked a son in his early years, so he adopted Xiao Zhengde, Xiao Hong's son, as his heir. Later, when Xiao Tong was born, he made Xiao Tong the crown prince (because of his posthumous title Zhaoming, he was called Prince Zhaoming). Xiao Tong did something that had a profound impact on the literary world of later generations, which was to organize a group of literati to compile and select 30 volumes of "Selected Works" (known as "Selected Works of Zhaoming" in the world). "Selected Works" contains a total of 514 important works by 130 writers from the pre-Qin Dynasty to the early Liang Dynasty. It is the earliest existing collection of poems and essays in my country and preserves important information for future generations to study the literary history of these seven to eight hundred years. An old proverb goes: "A bad "Selected Works" means only half a scholar." During the Tang and Song Dynasties, "Selected Works" became a must-read for scholars to study poetry and seek fame. As an quintessential anthology of literary works, it has been taken more seriously by people and developed into a specialized discipline - "Selected Literature". It is said that Liangzhou in Xuanwu Lake is where Prince Zhaoming led the literati to compile the "Selected Works". ——"Celebrities and Nanjing"

\u6c34\u4f53.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: One of the places where the Japanese army committed atrocities was that the Japanese army not only robbed things, but I also saw them kill a large number of people with machine guns. [The following is a picture to illustrate] It was half a month after the Japanese arrived. I saw a massacre by the Japanese army near the house where we lived. I remember it very clearly, it was 8 o'clock in the morning. I saw the Japanese army letting a thousand people who had taken refuge kneel on the embankment of the Qinhuai River, and firing diagonally from both sides with machine guns like a pincer attack. Men and women were seen falling slowly. There was a hut where crops were piled in the field under the embankment, and I hid there. The victims were not people from this village, they seemed to be ordinary civilians who had fled from the mountains. The cries of people and the sound of machine gunfire came over. Machine gunfire continued for dozens of minutes. I don’t know what happened to the body. I didn’t go near that place because I was afraid. ——"The Battle of Nanjing"

\u6c34\u4f53.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: It was originally an ordinary dry well in Hualin Garden. Because Empress Chen and her beloved concubine hid in it, it became a well that warned future generations of the country's subjugation. Empress Chen spent all day banqueting with her concubines, officials and scholars in Hualin Garden, often staying up all night. They once created some works such as "Linchun Music" and "Yushu Backyard Flowers", which later generations called "sounds of national subjugation". When the Sui army came to Jiankang City in 589 AD, Empress Chen and his beloved concubines hid in a dry well beside Jingyang Tower in Hualin Garden. They were all captured alive by the Sui soldiers. Therefore, this well is also called "Shame Well" or "Rouge Well." - "Nanjing History"

\u6c34\u4f53.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: During the Three Kingdoms period, Wulong Pond was named Qingshui Datang and Furong Pond. It is said in the Jin Dynasty that there are four springs in the lake, which gush all year round. On June 19th of a certain year, four black dragons were playing in the water around the spring. From then on, Wulong appeared on time every year, and Wulong Lake got its name from this. A few years later, the four springs disappeared one after another, and the Wulong Lake did not reappear, but the Wulong Lake is still beautiful. --Baidu Encyclopedia

\u6c34\u4f53.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: The origin of Jade Rabbit Spring is related to Qin Hui, who is known as "China's most treacherous official". When Qin Hui was studying in the Confucius Temple Academy, he saw a white rabbit entering the ground one night. He sent people to dig at the place guided by the jade rabbit. As soon as he dug 1 feet away, he found a spring here with clear water. After Qin Hui passed the imperial examination, he sent people to dig and build wells, and personally wrote "Jade Rabbit Spring" in seal script. ——Chinese Confucius Temple

\u6c34\u4f53.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: During the Hongwu period of the Ming Dynasty, the area of Xuanwu Lake shrank again, to about one-third of that of the Southern Dynasties. Zhu Yuanzhang built the city wall of Nanjing and initially built the Taiping Embankment outside the Taiping Gate, which caused the lake water to flow from the foot of the western foot of Zhongshan Mountain to the west bank of the Taiping Embankment. ——"Nanjing Architecture of the Ming and Qing Dynasties"

There are many records of rooster crow in the works of Tang and Song Dynasty poets such as Wen Tingyun and Li Shangyin. "Cockcrow Dai" Ma Zhichun is five miles from Taicheng to Qingxi, and Dai is west of Qingxi. Shooting pheasants towards the sun is only due to greed, and the rooster crows when there is always a deficit. The harrier is of little use. Who dares to judge the feathered harrier when it comes to pay tribute? That is to say, there must be a sense of discipline when traveling in the fields, how to be wild and crazy in the morning and evening.

\u6c34\u4f53.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: The scenery of Longjiang Pass is magnificent and beautiful, and literati often write about it. In the same boat, Chu and his guests wake up and face each other, sitting and waiting for all kinds of emotions to come into being in the spring.

\u6c34\u4f53.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《建康实录》、《东城志略》、《景定建康志》
\u6c34\u4f53.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: During the Six Dynasties, Yundu was excavated. In the first year of Huanglong (229), Sun Quan moved the capital from Wuchang to Jianye, "erected eight gates of the capital and built it as the Taichu Palace, dug Qingxi in the east, transported Du in the west, and reached Qinhuai". During the Six Dynasties, "Nanjing's river system was utilized and transformed on a large scale for the first time." They successively excavated or dredged the Yundu from the Qinhuai River in the southwest of the city to Cangcheng in the capital city, the Dongqu in the east of the city (i.e. Qingxi Section), most of the tidal ditch in the north and west of the city, and the Yuanhuaitang in the south of the city (i.e. the Qinhuai River (Part of the project) and other rivers are due to the changes in the original ecological landscape due to the construction of the city. "These projects have played a very important role in the water conservancy and water transportation of Jianye." ——"Nanjing City Wall Chronicles"

\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《景定建康志》、《建康实录》、《至正金陵新志》
\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4eba\u7269: 太平、韦庄、蒋山、朱偰、萧衍
\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: The city of Nanjing held the groundbreaking ceremony for the "Sino-Japanese Cooperation to Repair the Nanjing City Wall", the Nanjing Municipal Bureau of Culture and others held the "Retrospective Exhibition of Nanjing's 50 Years of Cultural Relics", and the name of the Huomeng Tower comes from Du Fu's poem
\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Due to the early death of the prince Xiao Tong, Emperor Wu of Liang Dynasty did not make Xiao Tong's son Xiao Qi (pronounced: chá) the emperor's grandson as usual, but instead appointed Xiao Gang as the prince. This intensified the internal conflicts in the royal family. At this time, the powerful officials Hou Jing and Xiao Zhengde colluded to take the opportunity to raise troops and attack Taicheng, which was known in history as "Hou Jing's Rebellion". In March of the third year of Taiqing (549), when Taicheng was captured, Hou Jing's troops "plundered women's treasures" and massacred the people. The 86-year-old Xiao Yan was imprisoned in the Jingju Hall in Taicheng. "What he wanted in his heart was not given, and he was worried, angry and sick. On Bingchen in May, Emperor You died of despair." It is said that Emperor Wu of Liang had a bitter taste in his mouth during his illness and wanted to eat honey. Hou Jing refused to supply it, and Xiao Yan died shouting "Hehe". This is the story of Emperor Wu of Liang starving to death in Taicheng. ——"Celebrities and Nanjing"

\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4eba\u7269: 太平、陈霸先、方山、蒋山、袁粲、朱元璋、刘禹锡、葛亮
\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: The origin of Nanjing's place names is related to the works of literati, Jianye, Bao Zhao's poems, Shi Wansui lamenting the steepness of Stone City, "Ten Scenes of Jinling"
\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: In the present-day Nanjing, to the west of Hujulu and about three hundred meters east of the Ming Dynasty's Qingliang Gate, there is a cliff with a towering city wall built on top. The face of the cliff is mottled and rugged, resembling a ghost's face, hence it is known as the "Ghost Face City". This is the site of the Six Dynasties Stone City ruins, a provincial-level cultural relic protection unit in Jiangsu. According to expert investigations, some of the city bricks on the Ming city wall where the Ghost Face City is located are of an earlier era and are suspected to be relics of the Six Dynasties. However, due to the steep terrain of the area, more detailed archaeological work has not been carried out. Therefore, identifying this place as representing the ruins of the Stone City from the Six Dynasties era is not yet fully substantiated by evidence. In recent years, some experts have suggested that the Stone City built by Sun Quan should be in the vicinity of today's Caochang Gate, while others suggest it might be near Dinghuai Gate. - "Nanjing Ming and Qing Architecture"

\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Inside the west gate of Han Dynasty, Wu was the city of Ye. The place where drums were cast in the Wu Dynasty. This is a red earth mountain. There is Xiegong Dun in the north. "Shishuo": Xie Taifu climbed to Yecheng and thought far away leisurely. He had the ambition to reach the world, hence the name. Li Bai's poem "Visiting historical sites in Yecheng, there is still Xiegong Dun" refers to this. "Yu Di Ji Sheng": In the middle of Jiading, the commander-in-chief Huang Du built a city tower on Yecheng Mountain, which was a scenic spot in the county and fell into ruins for a long time. ——"Jinling Scenic Spots"

The central axis of the imperial city starts from Hongwu Gate in the south, passes through Chengtian Gate, Duanmen and Meridian Gate and ends at Fengtian Gate. There is a wide imperial road between Hongwu Gate and Chengtian Gate, with long corridors on both sides called Qianbu Corridor. On the east side of the imperial road, there are five central administrative agencies, including officials, households, rituals, soldiers, and industry. On the west side of the imperial road, there are the seats of the five military governors' offices: front, rear, left, right, and center. Outside the Meridian Gate, to the southeast is the Ancestral Temple for worshiping ancestors, and to the southwest is the Sheji Altar for worshiping gods. Inside the Meridian Gate is the Neiyu River, with the Nei Wulong Bridge above it. Crossing the bridge is the Fengtian Gate. ——"Nanjing Architecture of the Ming and Qing Dynasties"

\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u7406\u4f4d\u7f6e: Zhonghuamenwai West Street area, Qinhuai District

After the Sui soldiers defeated Yue City, Yue City may have been destroyed at the same time and could not be rebuilt since then. Therefore, Xu Song of the Tang Dynasty has called it " "Abandoned Yue City". ——"Nanjing Architecture of the Ming and Qing Dynasties"

The flying clouds and falling moon all become prophecies, and the eternal spring heart looks upon the emperor with sorrow.

The woman in a bun is galloping on her horse, and the soldiers are catching chickens with swords. The market in the mountain city is often closed, and the emperor's territory has become a border. The shining tiles of the yellow house were shattered into the mud.

In the pavilion, there is a tablet of the Qing Sheng Ancestor. , more than two feet high. ——"New Nanjing"

\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Many literati wrote about the scenic spots of Jinling

\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u7406\u4f4d\u7f6e: Top of Lion Mountain in Gulou District
\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: According to historical records, Zhu Yuanzhang, the first emperor of the Ming Dynasty, once set up his headquarters at Lion Mountain, and his commander defeated Chen Youliang, laying the foundation for the establishment of the Ming Dynasty. For this reason, Zhu Yuanzhang issued an edict to build the Yuejiang Tower on the top of the mountain, and he and Song Lian, a scholar of Hanlin University, each wrote a "Record of the Yuejiang Tower". However, the construction could not be started for some reason, so there is an ancient saying of "there is a record but no tower". explain. ——"History of Nanjing Temples"

\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《建康实录》、《金陵古今图考》、《景定建康志》
\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4eba\u7269: 方山、杨郡、陈文述、蒋山、太平、葛亮、张纮
\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Jiangsu Provincial Cultural Affairs Administration established Jiangning County Library in Jiangsu Province
\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: During the Spring and Autumn Period, Wu and Chu met each other. Chu established the city of Jinling, and Qin changed Jinling to Moling. During the Three Kingdoms period, Moling was the capital of Emperor Wu, and Moling was renamed Jianye. It was renamed Jiankang in the Jin Dynasty, Jiangning County in the Sui Dynasty, and Baixia in the Tang Dynasty. Taizu of the Ming Dynasty made his capital Baixia, and changed Baixia's name to Yingtianfu; Yongle moved to Yan, and called Yan Beijing, and Jinling was called Nanjing. In the early Qing Dynasty, it inherited the old Nanji from the previous Ming Dynasty and named it Jiangnan. When the Qing Dynasty declined, Hong Xiu all came here and it was called Tianjing. Yang Wu was divided into Shangyuan and Jiangning. At the beginning of the Republic of China, all prefectures, prefectures, and offices were abolished and all counties were changed to counties. Therefore, Shangyuan and Jiangning were merged into one, and only Jiangning County remained. ——"Jinling Magazine Jinling Magazine Sequel"

\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: "Singing the Unicorn" was introduced to Jiangning County, Jiangning County banned temple fairs and folk drama troupe performances, the New Fourth Army Jiangning County Anti-Japanese Government held a meeting of tens of thousands of people to celebrate the victory of the Anti-Japanese War, and the "Second Round (Drama) Performance of Repertoires Created by Professional Literary and Art Groups in Jiangsu Province" was held in Nanjing Held, "The Most Obedient Girl" and "Three Relatives" went to Beijing to participate in performances, the Jiangning County Library was completed and opened, the Nanjing Municipal Museum immediately carried out on-site protection and archaeological investigation work, and the "Jinling Spring · Xiaokangle" performance was held in the Jiangning County Development Zone Held, five experts including Zeng Zhaoxuan, deputy director of the Nanjing Museum, were invited to investigate the tombs of the founding emperor Li Min of the Southern Tang Dynasty and the emperor Li Jing discovered in Zutang Mountain, and the Jiangsu Provincial Cultural Affairs Administration established the Jiangning County Library of Jiangsu Province.
\u5b98\u65b9\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Jiangning County Office was built in the early Hongwu period of Ming Dynasty. It is the former site of Jiali Building in Southeast Song Dynasty. It is also the general manager's office on Jiqing Road in Yuan Dynasty. Today it is still the governor's office of Jiangning County. On the left side of the office is Qin Zhuangyuan Alley, named after the residence of the great scholar in Qin Jianquan Hall. Jianquan official came to serve as a lecturer for bachelors. His sons Chengen and Chengye were both prominent officials in the capital. In Chengye, Qi Zhi was appointed as the emperor's tutor. After his death, he was given the title of Minister of the Ministry of Rites and was given the posthumous title Wenyi. Later generations regard their home as a shrine. ——"Nine Types of Jinling Suozhi"

Having a country and a family is just a dream, being a dragon or a tiger is nothing.

"Jiming Temple's Thoughts on Qi Jingjiang" Li Yu Jinling's autumn atmosphere is full of green, don't hate to be surprised by the full temple. Thousands of miles of mountains and mountains pity the long-term guest, and I sigh after taking the wrong road for a year. Discussing who has the same tune as the bright moon in his heart, he only tastes the wine and the clear breeze by himself. I would like to ask my old traveling companions whether the laughing songs have ever been desolate.

Huangfu Shanye Temple casts a bell at dusk, and the Zen lantern shines brightly across the water. I came looking for a place to grow sesame seeds, but I saw peaches and peanuts. The top is surrounded by Qingzhang, and the sky is far away from Chicheng. Sitting and talking about the theory of emptiness and color, there is only the feeling of staying in the face.

In the 14th year of Hongwu (1381), he moved the original temple to the current site of Linggu Temple. The terrain is a valley between "mountains on the left and steep ridges on the right" , it can be said that the mountains have spiritual energy and the valleys have combined water, so it is named "Linggu Zen Temple". The Wuliang Hall (also known as Wuliang Hall) built of bricks and stones houses the deity of Wuliangshou. The plaque of Linggu Temple was given by Zhu Yuanzhang, who also named the temple "Linggu Zen Temple".

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: In the seventeenth year of Hongwu in the Ming Dynasty (1384), Zhu Yuanzhang, the emperor of the Ming Dynasty, issued an edict to rebuild the palace and named it "Chaotian Palace". Chaotian Palace in the Ming Dynasty was, on the one hand, a dojo where royal nobles burned incense, prayed for blessings, and worshiped Taoist gods. On the other hand, it served as a place for civil and military officials to practice the etiquette of worshiping the emperor before the three major festivals (Spring Festival, Winter Solstice, and the emperor's birthday). This is a place where the officials’ children learn the etiquette of Hajj before attacking. ——"Nanjing Architecture of the Ming and Qing Dynasties"

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: "Visitable places to climb in Nandu", five kinds of "three wonders" in Jinling, and 20 scenic spots in Jinling
\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Qingliang Temple in the Tang Dynasty was an important place for scholars to realize Zen. "Visiting Qingliang Temple" (Excerpt) Tang Yanqian's white clouds and red trees linger on the road, and the ancient palace corridors line up one after another. To the south you can see the water and the Taoye Ferry, and to the north you can see the mountain pillowing the Stone City.

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Pagoda, Tianxi Temple, Changgan Temple, Jinling, Zhonghua Gate, Changganli, Yuhua Road, Jingcheng, Qinhuai, Qinhuai River, Tianjie Temple, Zhongshan
\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Dabaoen Temple is the oldest Buddhist temple in Nanjing. For more than a thousand years, it has been repeatedly demolished and rebuilt, and its name has been changed many times. It was destroyed by fire in the sixth year of Yongle in the Ming Dynasty (1408). In the tenth year of Yongle (1412), Zhu Di, the emperor of the Ming Dynasty, ordered the Ministry of Works to rebuild it on the original site. In the name of commemorating Ming Taizu and Queen Ma, Chengzu of the Ming Dynasty ordered the Ministry of Works to rebuild it here to repay his kindness. In accordance with the palace regulations, more than 100,000 craftsmen from all over the world were recruited to build the temple and the nine-story glazed pagoda, which cost 2.5 million taels of grain and silver and millions of dollars. It took 19 years to complete. "Based on the inner map of the Great Hall, a nine-level five-color glazed pagoda was built, which is called the First Pagoda, and the temple is called the Dabaoen Temple."

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: “The best place to board in Nandu”
\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: famous temple in the Six Dynasties, it got its name from the Taoist Taoist preaching here. According to records in "Jinling Buddhist Temple Chronicles", in the early years of the Eastern Jin Dynasty, Shili Midoluo, a monk from the Western Regions, came south to visit Jiankang. He was respected by Prime Minister Wang Dao, so he gave lectures in the temple. Because he sat in a high place when preaching, he was respectfully called "Gaozao Taoist", and people at that time also named the temple "Gaozao". According to another theory, Taoist Gaozuo later died in Jiankang and was buried in this temple. Emperor Yuan built a tomb for his tree tower, so the temple was renamed Gaozuo Temple. ——"History of Nanjing Temples"

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u7406\u4f4d\u7f6e: South foot of Zutang Mountain, Jiangning District
\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: The temple was rebuilt in the Ming Dynasty and was also called Zutang Temple. The temple is built on the mountain and is large in scale. From the foot of the mountain to the mountain gate, there is a long ancient road with towering trees, leaves blocking the sun, and temples and monks' houses scattered in an orderly manner. According to the "Jinling Brahma Temple Chronicles": the main halls in the Zutang Temple in the Ming Dynasty include the King Kong Hall, the Heavenly King Hall, the Buddha Hall, the Thousand Buddha Hall, the Guanyin Hall, the Huayan Hall, the Water and Land Hall, etc. Except for the Guanyin Hall, which has three couplets, all the other halls have five couplets. There are also a three-story fifteen-couplet meditation hall, a second-story ten-couplet dining hall, a five-couplet kitchen and a teahouse. The temple site covers an area of more than 240 acres, with more than 500 acres of fields, mountains and ponds. Youqi Temple also governed several small temples nearby during the Ming Dynasty. In front of the temple, there is a beamless hall, cloisters, crossing bridges and other buildings. ——"History of Nanjing Temples"
During the Hongwu period of the Ming Dynasty, it was renamed Qingliang Temple and Qingliang Baoen Temple, but was later destroyed. ——"The Origin of Nanjing Place Names"

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Many literati wrote about the scenic spots of Jinling
\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: In the Yuan Dynasty, a statue of Guanyin was carved in Guangzhai Temple. Zhao Mengfu and Guan Zhongji, the famous calligraphers and painters of the Yuan Dynasty, once painted statues of Guanyin in the temple and carved them into stone. ——"Nanjing Architecture of the Ming and Qing Dynasties"

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Wang Anshi visited Zan Yuan, Liu Xie dreamed of Confucius, and Liu Xie became a monk
\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: There are many inscriptions on the cliffs of Dinglin Temple by famous scholars from the Song Dynasty and inscriptions from the Southern Song Dynasty on the cliffs of Dinglin Temple. It is located on a cliff near the ruins of Dinglin Temple in Zixia Cave at the southern foot of Zhongshan Mountain in Nanjing. There are 5 stone carvings inscribed by Lu You, Han Wujiu and others in the Southern Song Dynasty. Lu You's inscription was originally located on the wall of Zhaowenzhai where Wang Anshi was studying. During the first to sixth years of Qiandao in the Song Dynasty (1165-1170), the monks of the temple moved it and carved it on the cliff. ——"Nanjing Cultural Chronicles Volume 2"

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u7406\u4f4d\u7f6e: Based on "The Complete Map of Jinling Province Newly Surveyed by Lu Shi Xuetang", supplement the positioning of "Shangjiang County Chronicles" in 1874 and "Jinling Magazine" in 1908
\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Guanyin Pavilion is located in Jiming Temple and is a must-visit place for literati. It was rebuilt in the sixth year of Tongzhi (1867). The scale was smaller than that of the Ming Dynasty. The temple monk Xichi and others raised funds to build the Guanyin Pavilion (also known as Guanyin Tower). There is a statue of Guanyin Bodhisattva sitting reversely (facing north) in the pavilion. The couplet on the Buddhist niche is: "Ask the Bodhisattva why he is sitting upside down. He hates all living beings and refuses to look back." In the 20th year of Guangxu (1894), Zhang Zhidong, the governor of Liangjiang, commemorated Yang Rui, one of the six gentlemen of the Reform Movement of 1898, at the former site of Hanxu Pavilion in the South Tang Dynasty on the left side of Guanyin Pavilion. A building was built on it, and he personally inscribed the building with the title "Huo Meng Building" based on Du Fu's poem about Zhang Zhidong that Yang Rui once sang about Zhang Zhidong. In the early years of the Republic of China, monks Shi Shou and Shi Xia built Jingyang Tower next to Huomeng Tower. At this time, Jiming Temple was still small in scale. Apart from the above-mentioned buildings, there were only more than ten Zen rooms in the temple. ——"Nanjing Architecture of the Ming and Qing Dynasties"

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u7406\u4f4d\u7f6e: Positioning based on the "New Map of Nanjing City in the 1930s"
\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: "The temple is located about two miles outside the Taiping Gate in Kyoto. According to legend, it is also an ancient Huazang nunnery. There is no monument to test, and we don't know when it was founded. It has been abandoned for a long time, but the foundation site still exists. The situation is majestic and the most scenic spot. Monk Miaoqing has gathered many people to support him. , Dove workers gathered wood, killed thatch and cut thorns, and all the halls and verandas were built accordingly. The bells and fish resounded, and the incense accumulated. The domes were lined with pines and cypresses. On June 18, the fifth year of Jingtai, the petition was submitted , I was given the title of Buddhist temple, and I was given the collection of scriptures and edicts to protect you forever. In August of the first year of Tianshun." - "Jinling Buddhist Temple Chronicles"

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Tianjie Temple has been greatly damaged in wars in modern times. From the poems of Jiao Xun of the Qing Dynasty, we can see the decline of the Qing Dynasty at that time: "In the past, when I recited Qingqiu Ci, I knew the name of Tianjie Buddhist Temple. Hongwu founded this temple and established it, and famous people first lived there. I visited Jinling Eleven times, this is the first time I have found this place. There are still dragon and tiger inscriptions on the walls, and there is a suspected fox charm on the door. The grass is deep and dew is found asking the village children, and the old monks are barefoot and offering tea. Small trees are sparsely stretched out of the eaves, and there is no sign of poison on the steps. Breeze." - "Nanjing Architecture of the Ming and Qing Dynasties"

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Nanjing's historic sites are commonly seen in ancient poetry. The river in front of me is waiting for a spoonful, and the monks are talking to me about the West. Chenla of the Netherlands had been paying tribute for a long time, but his ambition was not enough to satisfy Emperor Wen of the Ming Dynasty. The remaining ministers were sent to Yuanyuan, and the Ge boat pointed directly at the southwest wilderness. From the journey, bones are thrown to the shore, and when I return from nine deaths, my head is frosty. The kings of small and small balls in the Communist Party of China will gather together, and the pearls and elephants will return to their full glory. There are countless rare treasures...

\u5bfa\u89c2\u7960\u5e99.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: The God of Optimism is a supporting facility for the Temple of Heaven, and the God of Optimism holds music and dances in preparation for the great sacrifice to heaven and earth. It is listed as one of the forty-eight scenic spots in Jinling, and the scene is called "The Immortal Capital of Divine Music". It was originally called Zhenwu Emperor's Palace and was dedicated to the Taoist "Zhenwu Emperor". ——"Nanjing Ming Forbidden City"

\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4eba\u7269: 萧道成、桓玄、柳元景、谢安
\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: "Ten Scenes of Jinling", many literati praised the scenic spots of Jinling
\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: During the reign of Emperor Jian Wen, the popular Huan Wen controlled the military and political power. He had coveted the throne many times, but he failed to succeed because of the clever and witty efforts of Xie An and Wang Tanzhi. After Emperor Jian Wen died, Xie An took advantage of Huan Wen's absence in Jiankang to support Sima Yao as emperor. Huan Wen resented this and led a large group of troops back to Jiankang, and appointed Xie An and Wang Tanzhi to welcome him at the new pavilion. This was nothing less than another "Hongmen Banquet". Wang Tanzhi panicked and asked Xie An for advice. Xie An looked calm and said calmly: "The fate of the Jin Dynasty depends on this group." When he saw Huan Wen, Wang Tanzhi was sweating profusely and frightened. In his panic, he actually held the wat board upside down. Xie An took his time and sat at the table calmly, and said seriously to Huan Wen: "I heard that the duty of the princes is to guard the four sides. Why are you pressing the border with troops and your swords drawn?" Huan Wen was speechless and speechless, so he had to withdraw. ambush. Xie An then raised a toast with Huan Wen. During the banquet, the wind blew up the curtain, revealing Huan Wen's counselor Xi Chao who was hiding behind the tent and eavesdropping. Seeing this, Xie An smiled slightly and said, "Xi Sheng is really a guest at the scene." With one sentence, Xi Chao was filled with shame and ashamed. Huan Wen also blushed and felt extremely embarrassed. Huan Wen knew that he could not overpower Xie An morally, nor could he surrender by force. Xie An represented a group of aristocratic families who supported the Jin Dynasty. Huan Wen did not dare to act rashly, so he had to suppress his anger and shake hands to make peace. The Jin Dynasty relied on Xie An's wit and bravery to survive. ——"Celebrities and Nanjing"

\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: The "Wu Jingzi's Former Residence Exhibition Hall" has been completed and is open to the public, anecdotes about scenic spots in the Six Dynasties, works related to the Taoye Ferry in name only, the origin of Nanjing place names related to the works of literati, 20 scenic spots in Jinling, 23 "Jinling Scenery Poems" written by Wu Jingzi, "Ten Scenes of Jinling"
\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Taoye Crossing is named after Wang Xianzhi's poem "Taoye". Tao Ye Ge Wu Sheng song title. Chen Zhijiang of the Southern Dynasties (557-589) wrote in "Ancient and Modern Music Records": "The Peach Leaf Song was written by Prince Jing (named Xianzhi, 344-386 AD) of the Jin Dynasty. Taoye, the name of Zi Jing's concubine, Because of deep love, I sang it." "Book of the Sui Dynasty·Five Elements Chronicles" says: "In the Chen Dynasty, the king of popular songs in the south of the Yangtze River Xian wrote the lyrics of "Peach Leaf", saying: "Peach leaves become peach leaves, and there is no need to use a carabiner to cross the river, but there is nothing to cross." Bitter, I will welcome you.'" Therefore, there is a stone monument of the ancient ferry site of "Taoye Ferry" standing on the bank of the Qinhuai River in Nanjing. ——"Nanjing Cultural Chronicles Volume 1"

Wuma Crossing, as one of the forty-eight scenic spots in Jinling, the "Land of Transformation of Dragons", appeared many times in the writings of poets in the Song Dynasty. "Five Horse Crossing" What happened to Yang Bei, King Qi Zhong of Jinling, and Langya opened his mouth to occupy Jiangdong. The people in the boat suddenly saw a storm rising, and once the dragon flew among the five horses.

\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u7406\u4f4d\u7f6e: Inside the Ming Dynasty City Wall in the southwest of Gulou District
\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: The old pavilion of the Eastern Wu Dynasty was rebuilt in the Eastern Jin Dynasty. It was one of the "Three Pavilions of the Capital" at that time and an important military post. The Marquis of Donghun was in the East Palace, discussing the possibility of rebellion. He sent people to go up to the house to look, and saw that the Zhenglu Pavilion was on fire. ——Xu Song's "Records of Jiankang"

\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Since ancient times, it has been the choke point for entering and exiting the ancient city of Jinling. It is said that during the Song Dynasty, there were two archways made of blue bricks built in the north and south of the town, with the word "Guxiong" embedded on them. Therefore Banqiao is also called "Guxiong". ——"Nanjing Architecture of the Ming and Qing Dynasties"

The Emperor is about to leave, and his heart is moved when the Suzaku flies by - "Ten Miles of Qinhuai Bridge Taoist Records"

Zhenhuai Bridge was built in the Yangwu period of the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms. It has a history of more than a thousand years. It is the only way to go from the Imperial Street of the Southern Tang Dynasty to Changqianli outside the South Gate. Bridge) and the south gate are both on the north-south central axis. It was rebuilt many times in the Song Dynasty. When Shi Zhengzhi, the stayer in the Southern Song Dynasty, rebuilt it in the fifth year of Qiandao (1169), it was widened by one foot. In the fourth year of Baoyou (1256), the bridge collapsed and Ma Guangzu, the stayer, rebuilt it. It was destroyed by fire two years later and was rebuilt again. . --Baidu Encyclopedia

Shicheng Bridge, built in the early Ming Dynasty, is located on the moat outside Shicheng Gate. In the seventh year of Guangxu (1881), Shicheng Bridge was rebuilt. ——"Nanjing City Wall Chronicles"

\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Xiaoling Mausoleum of the Ming Dynasty is the mausoleum where Zhu Yuanzhang, the founder of the Ming Dynasty, and his queen are buried together. Later generations come here to pay their respects. "Visiting the Xiaoling Mausoleum" Xu Wei has been an old man for two hundred years and came to Xijing in despair. The tired donkey is worried about the official in the narrow road, and he has a torn hat and a blue shirt to pay homage to the Xiaoling Mausoleum. The head of the pavilion lifted his horse, and Long Live the Bridge Mountain began to welcome the dragon. At that time, my career was hard to come by, so I relied on the official's speaking and listening.

\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: The geographical location of Emperor Wu's mausoleum was important in the Ming Dynasty. "Shanling Kao": The mausoleum of Emperor Wu was located in Bali south of Zhongshan Mountain. Its old name was Sunlinggang - "Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum"

This place is the tomb of King Bangni, which is closely related to it and has scientific research value. The tomb of King Bangni is located at the southern foot of Wugui Mountain in Dongxianghua Village outside Andemen. The owner of the tomb is Manarajananai, Sultan II of the ancient Bangni Kingdom (now Brunei). The tomb of King Bangni faces south, with a pond in front and a pair of cow-headed palaces in the distance. It is surrounded by mountains on three sides in the east, west and north. From the perspective of "Feng Shui", which is historically important, the place condenses air and wind, and is surrounded by mountains and water. It is a "feng shui treasure land". The shrine of King Bangni's tomb is in an arc shape. From the tomb to the south, there are stone carvings of military commanders, stone tigers, stone sheep, stone horsemen, stone horses, stone pillar foundations, etc. Going south and turning eastward, there are "Hengni Kingdom is respectful". "King's Tombstone". ——"Nanjing Architecture of the Ming and Qing Dynasties"

\u5efa\u7b51.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Li Bai drank and composed poems, Wu Jingzi composed 23 "Poems on Pictures of Jinling Scenery", 20 Jinling Scenic Spots, "Jinling Ten Scenes", comparison of the Republic of China version of "Jinling Forty-Eight Scenes" and the Qianlong version of 48 scenes, the origin of Nanjing place names and the works of literati
\u5efa\u7b51.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: When Li Bai was 42 years old, he responded to the imperial edict and went to Chang'an to worship the Imperial Academy. He was soon slandered and "returned with gold" and roamed around again. From 747 to 750, he lived in Jinling most of the time. In 754, he and Wei Wan went boating in the Qinhuai River, and wrote poetry and manuscripts to support each other, which was later compiled into "Li Hanlin Collection". The famous "Climbing the Phoenix Tower of Jinling", "Three mountains are halfway to the blue sky, and two waters divide the Bailuzhou", which has become a good saying that has been passed down through the ages. This poem was written in Nanjing around this period. The last two sentences, "The clouds can always cover the sun, and the absence of Chang'an makes people sad" vividly express his depression after being hit politically. ——"Celebrities and Nanjing"

\u751f\u6d3b\u8bbe\u65bd.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: In the Qing Dynasty, there were Confucian temples and government schools along Chengxian Street. Before the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom, Nanjing's Confucian temples and private schools were located in the Chengxian Street area south of Jilong Mountain. ——"Nanjing Architecture of the Ming and Qing Dynasties"

\u751f\u6d3b\u8bbe\u65bd.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: In the Qing Dynasty, due to a case, the street was renamed Lu Fei Lane. It was named after the ninth concubine of Emperor Shizong of the Ming Dynasty, the Lu family, lived here. The streets here are paved with neat stone slabs, the streets are clean and the market is prosperous. This street was originally a dirt road with pits and bumps. Every time it rained, it was muddy and difficult to walk. If there was no rain for three days, the dust would fly, and pedestrians and shopkeepers would complain. During the reign of Guangxu, a case changed the appearance of the street. Old Nanjing residents still use this incident as a joke when enjoying the cool weather. ——"The Origin of Nanjing Place Names"

\u751f\u6d3b\u8bbe\u65bd.\u5730\u7406\u4f4d\u7f6e: Based on "The Complete Map of Jinling Province Newly Surveyed by Lu Shi Xuetang", supplement the positioning of "Shangjiang County Chronicles" in 1874 and "Jinling Magazine" in 1908
\u751f\u6d3b\u8bbe\u65bd.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: During the Qing Dynasty, some people visited Guiyuntang and wrote essays describing the unique scenery around it. Mei Zengliang's "Travel to Xiaopan Valley": Jiangning Fucheng ends at Lulong Mountain in the northwest. I tried to find Xiaopan Valley, but when I got there, the natives probably said it didn't exist. But the sky is covered by big bamboos, and there are many divergent roads, as wide and narrow as one, making it endless to explore. Hearing the sound of dogs, he rushed towards them, but no one was seen. After cooking five bushels of rice, we arrived at the temple and called Guiyuntang. The land was spacious and the residents made Gui Gui their industry. There is a small grass path next to the temple, and when you go out to the south, you fall into a large valley. There are big osmanthus trees on the four mountains, and they are like a large bowl with an empty sound inside, and there is no way to escape. There is no sound, but the ears are always full of hearing. The deep water accumulates and ends at the foot of the mountain. Go north from the temple to Lulong Mountain, where there are valleys and ridges, like the shape of a well stove, gums and palates. ——"Jinling Scenic Spots"

\u751f\u6d3b\u8bbe\u65bd.\u5730\u540d\u63cf\u8ff0: Jinling University was built for the United States, but in traditional Chinese architectural style, reflecting the blend of Chinese and Western cultures. With the North Building as the center, there are more than ten buildings including the Cundong Building, the Two Buildings, the Science Museum, the Auditorium, the Library, and the Dormitory Building, with an incompletely symmetrical layout. Its single shape is rigorous and symmetrical, with gray tube tiles and thick walls of blue bricks. Except for the earthy red color and detailed brick carvings, there are no carved beams or painted buildings. It is densely wooded, plain and natural, simple and quiet. ——"Nanjing Architectural Art of the Republic of China"

Yingtianfu (now Nanjing). In 1364, Zhu Yuanzhang proclaimed himself King of Wu here and granted hundreds of officials. In 1368, Zhu Yuanzhang proclaimed himself emperor, the country was named Daming, Jianyuan Hongwu, Yingtianfu was named Nanjing, and Kaifeng was named Beijing. Regarding when Nanjing was called the "capital" and "capital" during the Hongwu period, most later generations cited the "History of the Ming Dynasty" as the basis: Hongwu "changed Nanjing to the capital in the first month of the eleventh year." "In March of the Bingshen year of Taizu, he said 'Yingtianfu' In August of the first year of Hongwu, the capital was established, and it was called Nanjing. In the eleventh year, it was called the capital. In the first year of Yongle, it was still called 'Nanjing'." In fact, in January 1368 AD, Zhu Yuanzhang regarded Yingtian City as the "capital" and "capital" of the new dynasty. " has become a fact and was also used in early Ming Dynasty documents and Zhu Yuantan's many edicts. For example: "Ding Weishuo in the second month of the first year of Wu, the expansion of the capital city was completed..."; especially in the first month of the first year of Hongwu, what Zhu Yuanzhang said in the "Edict of Accession" "...established Taishe and Taiji in the capital" has actually been Nanjing's status as the capital was announced; on Wuyin, the first month of the first year of Hongwu, "Fang Guozhen came to the capital to see everyone...". ——"Nanjing Ming Forbidden City"
